mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.14%), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, supporting elevated equity valuations. As long as the policy rate stays at the effective lower bound, forward S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon should skew positive relative to historical baseline due to persistent yield-seeking capital flows into risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.14 as of t-1mo, near zero lower bound
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy / post-crisis liquidity regime
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX exhibits negative mean 60d returns during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% for 60% or more of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE of at least 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals improving labor market conditions and household income prospects, which raises consensus earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by >= 0.2pp over trailing 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=8.8 as of t-1mo, declining from cycle peak of 10.0 in late 2009
failure_condition: Mean 20d forward SPX return conditional on the UNRATE decline trigger is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05) across the walk-forward sample, or hit rate of positive returns falls below 50% in triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above 3% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains anchored below 0.25%, negative real policy rates initially fuel risk-asset inflows and positive equity returns, but the joint condition also raises the probability of policy tightening expectations that can reverse the effect. The interaction of rising inflation with a fixed near-zero rate is the driver, not either series alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=223.49 as of t-1mo reflecting rising commodity-driven inflation amid QE2
regime_hint: quantitative easing / negative real rate regime
failure_condition: Interaction-triggered signal produces forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from (or lower than) the single-factor FEDFUNDS-only baseline, or Rank IC of the joint signal is not higher than the max of the two individual single-series IC values over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently depressed housing starts (HOUST) below pre-crisis norms reflect ongoing household balance-sheet deleveraging and weak residential investment, a major component of GDP. As long as HOUST remains far below its historical trend level, this signals structurally weak demand that drags on growth expectations and produces below-average S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=479.0 thousand units as of t-2mo, far below pre-2008 levels near 1500-2000
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns during periods of HOUST below 600k units are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) relative to full-sample mean, or the effect reverses sign in more than one non-overlapping 2-year subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and capacity utilization, which is a leading indicator of corporate earnings momentum. Positive INDPRO growth in the latest reading is associated with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent trading week as markets incorporate improving production data into near-term earnings revisions.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=93.5904 as of t-1mo, in a recovering uptrend since 2009 trough
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly INDPRO growth and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign concordance rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS monthly net change exceeds +200,000 jobs, this crossing signals a confirmed acceleration in labor market healing beyond the recent trend pace, which raises market confidence in the durability of the economic recovery and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=130738 thousand as of t-1mo, still below pre-crisis peak of ~138000
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following PAYEMS prints above +200k are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05) across the walk-forward sample, or triggered hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Real consumer demand strength is captured by the interaction of nominal retail sales growth (RSAFS) outpacing consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL). When RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth over the same period, real consumption is expanding, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon. If RSAFS growth is merely tracking or below inflation, the demand signal is illusory and the mechanism should not activate.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=389320 million as of t-1mo showing continued nominal sales growth
fact:CPIAUCSL=223.49 as of t-1mo showing rising price levels
failure_condition: Real retail sales growth signal (RSAFS minus CPIAUCSL growth) shows Rank IC with forward 60d SPX return below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the signal underperforms a naive RSAFS-only single-series baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle recovery characterized by still-elevated unemployment slack (UNRATE near 8.8%), continued positive real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals that the economy is expanding despite labor market slack, which compresses the equity risk premium as investors price in sustained recovery rather than double-dip recession, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon. In a late-cycle regime with low unemployment, the same GDP growth signal would carry less incremental information and the effect should weaken or disappear.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=13380.7 billion as of t-6mo showing continued expansion
fact:UNRATE=8.8 as of t-1mo indicating persistent labor slack
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with high unemployment slack
failure_condition: The positive association between GDPC1 growth and forward 60d SPX returns fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically during periods when UNRATE exceeds 7%, or the relationship shows equal or stronger strength when UNRATE is below 6%, contradicting the regime-conditional claim.
